rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Allocation Of Defendants Attorneys Fees And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 14, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT KATHRYN DEVINCENZI'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ALLOCATION OF DEFENDANTS ATTORNEYS FEES AND EXPENSES is DENIED. Each side to bear their own attorney's fees. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LUIS E. GONZALEZ VS. SAS C-STORES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudiciation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 14, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist regarding prior notice and foreseeability. The Court notes that the motion has not been timely served. In light of Plaintiff filing an opposition on the merits, the Court issues a ruling on the merits. However, Defendant's technical difficulties with service are not good cause for providing less that statutorily mandated notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
